citation: Bloomberg L.P. v. Bd. of Govs. of the Fed. Reserve Sys., 649 F. 3d 651, 657 (D.C. Cir. 2011)
source_document: Gonzalez v. Texas Taxpayers & Research Association, Civil No. 1:25-CV-00029 (S.D. Tex. Jan. 29, 2025)
court_finding: Court explicitly states this citation 'does not exist' as part of sanctions order for AI-generated brief containing fabricated citations.
context: Pittman cites Bloomberg L.P. v. Bd. of Govs. of the Fed. Reserve Sys., 649 F. 3d 651, 657 (D.C. Cir. 2011), Hogan v. AT&T, Inc., 917 F. Supp. 1275, 1280 (S.D. Tex. 1994), and Head v. Chicora Life Ctr., LLC, 290 F. Supp. 3d 883, 893 (S.D. Tex. 2018), which do not exist.
notes: Completely fabricated case. No case with this name exists at this citation. Part of AI hallucination in legal brief.

citation: Hogan v. AT&T, Inc., 917 F. Supp. 1275, 1280 (S.D. Tex. 1994)
source_document: Gonzalez v. Texas Taxpayers & Research Association, Civil No. 1:25-CV-00029 (S.D. Tex. Jan. 29, 2025)
court_finding: Court explicitly states this citation 'does not exist' as part of sanctions order for AI-generated brief.
actual_case_at_citation: United States Ex Rel. Green v. Washington, 917 F. Supp. 1275 (D.D.C. 1994)
context: Same passage as Bloomberg citation above - court identified as non-existent.
notes: Real citation (917 F. Supp. 1275) but completely wrong case name. The citation belongs to 'United States Ex Rel. Green v. Washington', not 'Hogan v. AT&T, Inc.' Our verifier correctly rejects this as NOT_FOUND because the name doesn't match.

citation: Shell Petroleum N.V. v. Republic of Costa Rica, 608 F. Supp. 2d 269 (S.D.N.Y. 2009)
source_document: Flowz Digital LLC v. Flowz Staffing LLC, Case No. 2:25-cv-00709-SB-PVC (C.D. Cal. May 5, 2025)
court_finding: Court states: 'after multiple search attempts, the Court is unable to locate Shell Petroleum N.V. v. Republic of Costa Rica, 608 F. Supp. 2d 269 (S.D.N.Y. 2009).'
context: Moreover, after multiple search attempts, the Court is unable to locate Shell Petroleum N.V. v. Republic of Costa Rica, 608 F. Supp. 2d 269 (S.D.N.Y. 2009). Plaintiff shall attach the case to its response to this order to show cause.
notes: Completely fabricated case. Court unable to locate after multiple searches. Citation lookup found a different case ('Faghri v. University of Connecticut') at 608 F. Supp. 2d 269.

citation: Butler Motors, Inc. v. Benosky, 181 N.E.3d 857 (Ind. Ct. App. 2021)
court_finding: Court states: 'The Wilcoxes also cite "Butler Motors, Inc. v. Benosky, 181 N.E.3d 857 (Ind. Ct. App. 2021)" in their Table of Authorities as appearing on page 3 and on page 17 of their brief, but the actual case citation is at 181 N.E.3d 304.'
actual_case_at_citation: Butler Motors, Inc. v. Benosky, 181 N.E.3d 304 (Ind. Ct. App. 2021)
context: The Wilcoxes also cite "Butler Motors, Inc. v. Benosky, 181 N.E.3d 857 (Ind. Ct. App. 2021)" in their Table of Authorities as appearing on page 3 and on page 17 of their brief, but the actual case citation is at 181 N.E.3d 304.
notes: Real case with wrong page number. Cited as 181 N.E.3d 857 but actual citation is 181 N.E.3d 304. Also note: extraction pipeline lost 'Butler' from party name, extracting as just 'Motors, Inc. v. Benosky'.

citation: TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter, 640 S.W. 2d 232, 237 (Tex. 1982)
source_document: Gonzalez v. Texas Taxpayers & Research Association, Civil No. 1:25-CV-00029 (S.D. Tex. Jan. 29, 2025)
court_finding: Court states: 'The Court could not locate a case entitled TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter, and the case at 640 S.W. 2d 232 is a wrongful foreclosure case captioned Ogden [v. Gibraltar Savings Ass'n].'
actual_case_at_citation: Ogden v. Gibraltar Savings Ass'n, 640 S.W.2d 232 (Tex. 1982)
context: TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter, 640 S.W. 2d 232, 237 (Tex. 1982), representing that the court "held that a plaintiff's allegations of defamatory statements made during business meetings and in internal company communications were sufficient to state a claim, even though the plaintiff could not provide the exact wording of the statements at the pleading stage." The Court could not locate a case entitled TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter, and the case at 640 S.W. 2d 232 is a wrongful foreclosure case captioned Ogden.
notes: Real citation (640 S.W. 2d 232) but completely wrong case name. The citation belongs to 'Ogden v. Gibraltar Savings Ass'n', not 'TIG Ins. Co. v. Carter'. Same source document as Bloomberg/Hogan/Head hallucinations.

citation: Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises, Inc., 962 F. Supp. 1162 (E.D. Pa. 1997)
source_document: Nora v. M.A. Transport, Inc. (N.D. Ill. Aug. 13, 2025)
court_finding: Court states: 'The citation "962 F. Supp. 1162" corresponds to Kenro, Inc. v. Fax Daily, Inc. rather than Gallagher.' Court also notes: 'Although Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises, Inc. is the name of a reported opinion issued by the United States Court of Appeals for the First Circuit, neither Gallagher nor Kenro discuss venue transfer or equitable tolling.'
actual_case_at_citation: Kenro, Inc. v. Fax Daily, Inc., 962 F. Supp. 1162 (S.D. Ind. 1997)
context: The first of these "suspicious" citations is Ms. Hamilton's citation to Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises, Inc., 962 F. Supp. 1162 (E.D. Pa. 1997). Ms. Hamilton cited Gallagher as a case in which "the Eastern District of Pennsylvania transferred a case filed in the wrong district rather than dismissing it." The citation "962 F. Supp. 1162" corresponds to Kenro, Inc. v. Fax Daily, Inc. rather than Gallagher.
notes: Real citation (962 F. Supp. 1162) but wrong case name. Gallagher v. Wilton Enterprises does exist as a First Circuit opinion, but at a different citation. The reporter number belongs to Kenro, Inc. v. Fax Daily, Inc. A hybrid hallucination: real case name + real citation number that don't go together.

citation: Gibbs v. Wright, 2020 WL 1877661, at *2 (E.D. Tenn. Apr. 15, 2020)
source_document: Hardy v. Ford Motor Co., Case No. 23-cv-12716 (E.D. Mich. 2025)
court_finding: Court states: 'Hardy's brief includes fake citations' and lists this citation alongside 'Moore v. Ford Motor co., 755 F.2d 510, 513 (6th Cir. 1985)'. Court adds: 'The Court suspects that the fake citations result from Hardy using generative artificial intelligence (AI).'
context: Making matters worse, Hardy's brief includes fake citations. ECF No. 59, PageID.413 (citing "Moore v. Ford Motor co.,755 F.2d 510, 513 (6th Cir. 1985)" and "Gibbs v. Wright, 2020 WL 1877661, at *2 (E.D. Tenn. Apr. 15, 2020)"). The Court suspects that the fake citations result from Hardy using generative artificial intelligence (AI).
notes: Confirmed fake citation. Court explicitly calls it fake. Extraction pipeline mangled this as 'In re Wright, 2020 WL 1877661' (wrong case name, lost court and date) due to PDF line break splitting the parenthetical. With the correct full citation, our verifier correctly returns NOT_FOUND.

citation: Lopez v. Bank of Am., N.A., No. 14-cv-2524, 2016 WL 4131149 (N.D. Cal. Aug. 3, 2016)
qc_finding: Confirmed hallucination in QC review (2026-02-21). Docket number and date do not match the real Lopez v. Bank of America docket the verifier matched.
xfail_reason: Lever 3 (docket#/WL contradiction penalty) not yet implemented. v0.3+Lever1 still VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY 0.85: name+court match a real Lopez/Bank of America docket, only the cited docket#, WL number, and date contradict. Name-token + temporal gates (Lever 1) don't catch it. Remove this xfail when Lever 3 lands.
notes: A real docket with a similar caption exists, but the cited docket number, WL number, and date belong to no real document. v0.2 scored it 0.65 on surname similarity alone — the motivating case for docket/date mismatch penalties (TODO Priority 1).

citation: Johnson v. Mitchell, 2:20-cv-1882, 2020 WL 5649609 (S.D. Ohio Sept. 23, 2020)
qc_finding: Confirmed hallucination in QC review (2026-02-22). Docket mismatch, name mismatch, inexact date — QC note: 'should be much much lower.'
xfail_reason: Lever 3 (docket#/WL contradiction) not yet implemented. Lever 2 (party-mismatch penalty + cap) eliminated the earlier party-mismatch matches (Scudder v. Mitchell opinion 0.50; Laile v. Mitchell docket): it now falls through to a real 'Johnson v. Mitchell' RECAP docket at VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY 0.42. Party names genuinely match, so only the cited docket# (2:20-cv-1882) and WL number (5649609) contradicting the matched docket can reject it. Remove when Lever 3 lands.
notes: v0.2 matched Laile v. Mitchell on defendant surname. Lever 2 killed that match; the residual is a same-name docket with a contradicting docket#/WL (Lever 3).

citation: Thompson v. Best, 989 N.E.2d 299 (Ind. Ct. App. 2013)
qc_finding: Confirmed hallucination in QC review (2026-02-21). Wrong court, not the same case name.
xfail_reason: Lever 3 (reporter-cite contradiction) not yet implemented. Lever 2 (party-mismatch penalty) eliminated the Thompson v. Thompson opinion match (0.625): it now falls through to a 'Thompson v. Best Buy Co.' RECAP docket at VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY 0.425. Party overlap is genuinely TRUE here because the cited defendant 'Best' is a substring of 'Best Buy', so the party penalty does not fire — only the cited reporter cite (989 N.E.2d 299) contradicting the matched docket can reject it. Remove when Lever 3 lands.
notes: v0.2 matched Thompson v. Thompson. Lever 2 killed that opinion match; the residual is a 'Thompson v. Best Buy' docket where 'Best' substring-matches 'Best Buy' (party overlap True), needing reporter-cite contradiction (Lever 3).
